_description: Sample monthly changelog data for February 2026 - Demonstrates the expected JSON structure for quarterly consolidation input

entries.regulation_name: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation
entries.title: ZSVR 2025 Minimum Standard Published
entries.description: The German Central Agency Packaging Register (ZSVR) has published the 2025 Minimum Standard for assessing the recyclability of packaging. This standard acts as a bridge to PPWR compliance, providing specific technical criteria for recyclability assessment including density thresholds for polyolefins, solubility parameters for adhesives, and guidance on acceptable additives. The standard provides clarity for manufacturers preparing for PPWR's general application in August 2026.
entries.executive_summary: German ZSVR standard provides interim technical guidance for PPWR recyclability assessment, offering a compliance bridge until August 2026.
action_items: Compare current packaging portfolio against ZSVR 2025 criteria
Identify packaging formats at risk of Grade D or E classification
Schedule supplier audits for non-compliant materials

entries.title: Municipal Fee Increases Effective - First Invoicing
entries.description: The Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg's municipal fee increases have taken effect on January 1, 2026. February 2026 represents the first invoicing cycle under the new rates. The Schmutzwassergebühr (foul water fee) and Niederschlagswassergebühr (rainwater fee) have both increased by approximately 3.3%. First invoices should be carefully audited to verify correct application of new unit rates.
entries.executive_summary: Hamburg wastewater fee increases of 3.3% now effective, with first invoices requiring careful audit to verify correct rate application.
action_items: Audit February 2026 utility invoices for correct rate application
Recalculate water efficiency project ROI with new rates

entries.title: Draft VerpackDG Advances - B2B EPR Framework Confirmed
entries.description: The draft Verpackungs-Durchführungsgesetz (VerpackDG) continues to advance through the legislative process. The key provision mandating Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for B2B packaging remains intact. The new levy of approximately €5 per tonne for non-system-participating packaging is confirmed in the latest draft. The law is expected for adoption in Q1 2026 with implementation in Q3/Q4 2026.

sources.title: New Draft of The VerpackDG - Analysis

entries.executive_summary: VerpackDG draft advances with B2B EPR mandate confirmed. New €5/tonne levy expected Q3/Q4 2026.
action_items: Quantify annual B2B packaging tonnage for Hamburg and Rietberg facilities
Evaluate authorized OfH (Organisation for Producer Responsibility) membership options
Review contracts with waste management providers for OfH positioning

entries.regulation_name: Supply Chain Due Diligence Act (LkSG) Amendment
entries.title: LkSG Reporting Obligation Abolished - Retroactive Effect
entries.description: The German Federal Cabinet has passed a law amending the Supply Chain Due Diligence Act (LkSG), abolishing the reporting obligation under § 10 (2) LkSG with retroactive effect from January 1, 2023. Companies are no longer required to submit annual reports to BAFA. However, all substantive due diligence obligations remain in force: risk management systems, annual risk analyses, policy statements, and preventive/remedial measures. BAFA retains authority for risk-based controls.

sources.title: Federal government abolishes reporting obligations under the LKSG

entries.executive_summary: LkSG reporting obligation abolished retroactively from January 2023. Substantive due diligence obligations remain fully in force.
action_items: Redirect BAFA reporting resources to CSDDD/CSRD preparation
Ensure continued compliance with substantive LkSG obligations
